Yasmin Nasser is a Java Developer with contact information including email (yasmin.nasser@email.com) and a GitHub profile (github.com/yasmin-nasser).

Yasmin Nasser received a Bachelor of Engineering in Information Technology from Vidyalankar Institute of Technology in June 2016, located in Goregaon, Maharashtra, India, and a Diploma in Information Technology from Thakur Polytechnic in May 2013, located in Mumbai, Maharashtra, India. She also obtained a High School Diploma from St. John’s Universal School in May 2010, also in Mumbai, Maharashtra, India.

From September 2016 to June 2018, Yasmin Nasser worked as a Java Developer at Tech Mahindra in Cairo, Egypt, where she applied change requests, performed requirement analysis, conducted dev unit testing, handled issues, and prepared documentation.

From September 2016 to August 2017, Yasmin Nasser developed an application called OR-Formatter at Tech Mahindra to process Geneva Modified Files (GMF) for BT clients, enhancing functionality and ensuring data accuracy.

From September 2017 to June 2018, Yasmin Nasser engineered an encapsulation middleware layer called STAA (Service Telephony Access Abstraction) at Tech Mahindra, implementing data transformation and processing XML/HTTP inputs.

Yasmin Nasser possesses skills in programming languages like Java/J2EE, Swift, and HTML, databases like MySQL, and tools and frameworks such as SVN, Jenkins, Hudson, Weblogic12c, Eclipse, and Xcode, along with software and OS proficiency in Android Studio, Oracle, Windows 10, and Mac (High Sierra).

Yasmin Nasser is a resourceful and technically adept Java Developer, steadily building a foundation in software engineering since graduating with a Bachelor of Engineering in Information Technology from Vidyalankar Institute of Technology in 2016. Her educational journey began earlier, with a Diploma in Information Technology from Thakur Polytechnic (2013) and a High School Diploma from St. John’s Universal School (2010), both in Mumbai, India – a testament to her consistent pursuit of knowledge. 

Her professional experience centers around a two-year tenure at Tech Mahindra in Cairo, Egypt, where she quickly proved her capabilities. She wasn’t simply writing code; she was actively involved in the full development lifecycle – from analyzing requirements and applying change requests to rigorous dev unit testing and meticulous documentation.  Yasmin demonstrated a knack for problem-solving, adeptly handling issues as they arose. 

During her time at Tech Mahindra, she wasn’t content with routine tasks. She spearheaded the development of OR-Formatter, an application designed to refine Geneva Modified Files for BT clients, prioritizing both functionality and data integrity.  She further showcased her architectural skills by engineering STAA (Service Telephony Access Abstraction), a middleware layer focused on data transformation and processing, skillfully navigating XML and HTTP inputs.

Yasmin’s toolkit is impressively broad. She’s fluent in Java/J2EE, with growing proficiency in Swift and HTML.  She’s comfortable working with MySQL databases and leverages tools like SVN, Jenkins, Hudson, Weblogic12c, Eclipse, and Xcode to streamline her workflow.  Her operating system expertise extends to Windows 10 and Mac (High Sierra), and she’s proficient in Android Studio and Oracle.  You can find her work and connect with her via GitHub at github.com/yasmin-nasser, or reach her directly at yasmin.nasser@email.com.
